ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report
Off Calendar Jun-16-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: James Bridgman, via Zoom, for and with FE Thibodeaux and Paz Rodriguez, Objectors; Mark Senick, via Zoom, for Manuela Ramirez, co-Trustee; Deb Fox, via Zoom, for Cres Ramirez, co-Trustee.

The court queries Mr. Bridgman whether he is objecting to the accounting in the Bypass Trust. Mr. Bridgman reports that he has not received an accounting since August of 2021 and does not know the status of the trust accounts since then but that he does not object to the accounting in the Bypass Trust. Mr. Senick reports of complying with court orders regarding accountings and has served Mr. Bridgman accordingly. Mr. Sencik reports of having additional accountings to file and will request distributions at the time of filing. The court points to its intent to reduce the 2018 trustee fees requested in the instant petition. Mr. Selnick offers advocacy on behalf of the trustee regarding the fees being requested.

The court takes the matters under submission.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Bastain. Not Reported.

MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: James Bridgman for Fe Thibodeaux and Paz Ramirez (Petitioners).
Deborah Fox for Cres Ramirez (Co-Trustee).

Ms. Fox stated that the Parties stipulated to working with Bette Epstein as the Referee to review the accounting, that the stipulation was filed timely, but courtesy copies were not submitted to the Court, that she submitted the courtesy copies on Friday and that they are just waiting for the Court to act. The Court spoke of CCP 638 and CCP 639, questions why not go under CCP 638 and stated that one is by stipulation of the Parties and one is by Court order. Mr. Bridgman stated that they did go under CCP 638. The Court noted that the parties then do not need a Court order. Mr. Bridgman stated that it was their understanding that it was required. The Petition to Enforce Settlement Agreement, etc. is continued to April 27, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. If the matter is not resolved, the Court will need to set the Parties up for trial and will require a joint status report at that time.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Anasse. Not reported.

MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: James Campbell for Fe Thibodeaux and Paz Ramirez.
Deborah Fox for Cres Ramirez (Co-Trustee).
Mark Senick for and with Manuela Ramirez (Co-Trustee).

The Court asks how the parties intend to proceed. Mr. Bridgman stated that this case has always been about getting information, that they get information at the last minute, but are not receiving all of it, and that maybe mediation will be helpful, but he needs enough time to get information. Mr. Senick stated that Mr. Bridgman has never formally asked for documents, that he does not feel mediation will be helpful, that they have had mediation before and Mr. Bridgman's Client tried backing out, and suggested that they be referred to a Referee to exam the accounting and report to the Court. Ms. Fox stated that they had a CPA prepare the accounting which shows all of the money accounted for, spoke of the costs and expense of having an evidentiary hearing, and that it makes sense to send this to a Referee. Mr. Bridgman stated that he has looked at the accounting, that he did not have an Accountant look at it and that they still do not have some statements. The Court strongly suggested to Mr. Bridgman to have the accounting looked at by an Accountant. The Petition to Enforce Settlement Agreement, etc. is continued to July 10, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

Petition To Address Objections To Accouintings And For An Accounting For 2014; And For Reimbursement Of Expenses Incurred By Paz Rairez On Behalf Of The Trust; And To Instruct The Trustees To Reimburse Paz Ramirez
Off Calendar Mar-01-2016 Continued To Apr-05-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mark Senick for and with (trustee/respondent) Manuela Ramirez. Deborah Fox for Cres Ramirez. Fe Thibodeaux & Paz Ramirez in Propria Persona.

The Court stated that we are here on the co-trustees petition to approve a settlement agreement signed by all parties, that there is a petition by the objectors, that there is a new petition set for April 5, 2016 to remove the trustee and for an accounting, that there is an objection to the petition to approve the settlement, that we need structure to resolve whether the settlement agreement will be enforced and asked if it is the trustees expectation to do an accounting. Mr. Senick stated that they have done yearly accountings, that the information is at the accountant's office now but that they are not sure of the time frame for completion. The Court asked that in regards to the objections to the settlement agreement, is it the trustees expectation that the issues can be resolved or will they need to file another petition or a motion, that it is inclined to continue the petitions to be heard on April 5, 2016 with the new petition and that it will give the parties a chance to file objections and responses. Ms. Ramirez stated that she is not clear on things. The Court stated that they need to get an attorney, that they are creating enormous expense, that the property and expenses in the Philipines will not be addressed here and that they need an attorney to explain the havoc they are wreaking. The petition to address objections to accountings and for an accounting for 2014, etc., and the petition for approval of settlement agreement and status report following court-ordered mediation were continued to April 5, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)

Petition To Address Objections To Accouintings And For An Accounting For 2014; And For Reimbursement Of Expenses Incurred By Paz Rairez On Behalf Of The Trust; And To Instruct The Trustees To Reimburse Paz Ramirez
Off Calendar Dec-08-2015 Continued To Jan-26-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Deborah Fox and Mark Senick, for and with Manuela Ramirez; petitioner Fe Thibodeaux; petitioner Paz Ramirez.

The Court asked if there is a 2014 accounting in the works. Mr. Senick replied that a 2014 accounting has been prepared and circulated to the beneficiaries for review. The Court stated that there are accounting issues, and asked if the parties had considered arnitration. Ms. Fox stated that the parties did try unsuccessfully to resolve their disputes through mediation in 2014, but that her Manuela Ramirez would be willing to try further mediation. Ms. Thibodeaux stated that the prior mediation involved matters in the Philippines. She did not agree to further mediation as the way to proceed. The Court stated that there is a program for pro bono mediation available through the Court, and that it would be limited to issues involving the trust would not include the Philippines issues. Mr. Senick stated that there had been objections by the petitioners that information had not been provided by Manuela Ramirez, but whenever information had been requested it was given to the petitioners either by him or Ms. Fox. The petitioners maintained their unwillingness for mediation. The Court stated that if the matter could not be mediated and was to proceed by litigation, the petitioners needed to amend their petition to make clearer their objections and the relief they are requeesting. The matter was continued to January 26, 2016 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204, in anticipation of amendments to the pbetition being filed and served.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not reported)
